Felix sleeps 7.5 hours, recovering from bartending shift and low energy from yesterday; sleep beat spans into next calendar day as required.

diary: Woke up early and knocked out a product shoot at the studio—nothing fancy, just some clean lighting work that paid the bills. Grabbed lunch at Café Solstice, their oat milk cappuccino hits different on Fridays. Headed home for a few hours and got lost in some gaming, which felt necessary after the morning hustle. The bar shift was wild though. Pride kickoff meant Capitol Hill was absolutely buzzing, and even though we're in First Hill, you could feel the energy radiating through downtown. People were in such good spirits, the drinks were flowing, and I found myself smiling more than usual while pouring. Picked up some chamomile tea on the commute home and just let the day settle. Sipped it slowly, didn't really scroll much—just sat with the warmth of it and the lingering buzz of the crowd. Felt good to be part of something celebratory, even from behind the bar.
thought_process: I structured my day around maximizing income from both gigs while protecting some downtime—the afternoon break was intentional because I knew the bar would be slammed. The Pride energy shifted my mood noticeably; normally Friday shifts are routine, but there was genuine joy in the room today that made the work feel less transactional. I could've pushed harder to shoot the street fair for portfolio work, but honestly, I needed the mental space more. The tea ritual at the end was about holding onto that good feeling rather than chasing productivity. Sometimes being present matters more than optimizing every hour.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
